title: Patient Mister Glass · the ledger
notes: Entries reveal when their token lands in GauntletState.lore_tokens_revealed (hosts emit on finished; conditional tokens per manifest). Schema matches pirate_summer/scrapbook.json.

entries.title: The file, closed
entries.blurb: Rain, a drive home, a kitchen, a ledger that didn't add. However you closed it, it closed. Mister Glass was patient the whole time. That was the unsettling part.

entries.title: The kitchen goes dark
entries.blurb: You accused. The file closes. Some doors don't reopen because you were right.

entries.title: Both of you know
entries.blurb: You cleared him. The file closes. Both of you know what the ledger said, and neither of you will ever mention it.

entries.title: CLOSE THE LEDGER
entries.blurb: Not innocence. Not guilt. Settled. The third verdict the game never tells you exists until you refuse the first two.

entries.title: The envelopes answer
entries.blurb: There was a second ledger. Pirate Summer's bulletin board has been quoting it for thirty years and calling it camp trivia.

entries.title: The silent evening
entries.blurb: A whole evening in the kitchen without one question. At the door: 'my wife used to do that. sit there while i cooked. not asking.' Come again.
